title: Structures, powers, and functions of Congress: foundational
description: Test your knowledge of about the structure, powers, and purpose of the US Congress!
topic_path: What powers does Congress have in the U.S. government?
Structures, powers, and functions of Congress: foundational

assessment_items.question_content:
**The power to confirm presidential appointments belongs to which part of the legislative branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Senate
The House of Representatives
It’s a shared power
It belongs to a different branch

assessment_items.question_content:
**The power to initiate a tax bill belongs to which part of the legislative branch? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The House of Representatives
The Senate
It’s a shared power
It belongs to a different branch

assessment_items.question_content:
**Does Congress set both the discretionary funding budget and the mandatory spending budget?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No, Congress only has power over the discretionary budget.
No, Congress only has power over the mandatory budget.
No, the executive branch is in charge of these budgets.
Yes, Congress sets both of these budgets.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one power that the House and the Senate share?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the power to declare war
the power to initiate impeachment proceedings
the power to ratify treaties
the power to initiate revenue bills

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is an example of mandatory spending?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Social Security payments
Defense spending
Education funding
Foreign aid

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who is the leader of the House of Representatives?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Speaker of the House
the President Pro Tempore
the Majority Whip
the chair of the Rules Committee

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is cloture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a maneuver that limits the amount of time for debating a bill in the Senate
a strategy to prevent a bill or another measure from reaching the Senate floor for a vote
a House procedure that  brings a bill out of committee and speeds up the lawmaking process for voting on a bill
a tactic to block a bill by holding the Senate floor and continuing to speak

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the name of the formal procedure that brings a bill out of committee and onto the House floor for a vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: discharge petition
filibuster
cloture
hold

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is an advantage that the majority party has in the Senate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The majority leadership can table a bill in committee.
They can bring of a partisan bill to a vote through unanimous consent.
They can stop a filibuster for legislation with 51 votes.
They can override a presidential veto.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is the Rules Committee in the House of Representatives powerful?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because it determines when and how a bill will be debated on the floor
because it decides who will be the Speaker of the House
because it can keep bills in committee
because it ensures that debates remain bipartisan

assessment_items.question_content:
**What role does the Rules Committee play in the lawmaking process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It controls if and when bills are heard on the floor.
It decides who becomes the next Speaker of the House.
It resolves differences in House and Senate versions of a bill.
It confirms all presidential nominations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the main function of the legislative branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to make laws
to declare executive orders
to uphold Supreme Court decisions
to control executive agencies

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is an example of pork barrel legislation in a House spending bill?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: \$398 million is set aside to build a bridge for a small community in Alaska.
\$180 million is set aside for competitive innovation and research education grants.
\$36 billion is set aside to pay for unemployment benefits.
\$44 billion is set aside for repairing highways across the country.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the main purpose of committees in the Senate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They prepare bills for the whole Senate to debate.
They confirm presidential appointments.
They table legislation.
They nominate and confirm the president of the Senate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is an example of how the minority party can slow down the policymaking process in the Senate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They can filibuster a bill.
They can perform cloture.
They can table a bill.
They can propose different rules for debate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a filibuster?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a tactic to block a bill by continuing to hold the floor and speak
a procedure that brings a bill out of committee and speeds up the lawmaking process
a maneuver that limits the amount of time for debating a bill before it goes to a vote
a strategy to prevent a bill or another measure from reaching the floor for a vote

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is pork barrel legislation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: laws that include federally-funded local projects that only benefit a specific district
laws created by the president without congressional approval
requirements that a state or local government must meet in order to receive federal money
federal grants that state and local governments can spend however they see fit

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes logrolling?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an agreement between two legislators to trade votes for each other's benefit
a process for bringing formal charges against a government official for alleged crimes or abuses of power
an alliance among political groups pursuing a common goal
a way for legislators to fund local projects in their district by adding small provisions to bigger bills

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the Committee of the Whole?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the group of all 435 members of the House
the group of all 100 members of the Senate
the entire group of legislators from both the Senate and the House
the majority party in the House of Representatives

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is discretionary spending?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the federal money that Congress must approve each year
the money that the government has already committed to spending
an agreement between two representatives to trade votes for each other’s benefit.
the allocation of federal funds for local projects
